prompt:
We do a Game of Life demo at every intern onboarding and I'm tired of the janky shell script we use — I want a proper Go package I can build terminal visualizers on. Module go-lifegrid, package lifegrid at the root, stdlib only. The pattern files we already have (see testdata/) are the input format: lines starting with '!' are comments, blank lines are ignored, every other line is a row of '.' (dead) and '#' (alive). Parse(data string, mode Mode) (*Grid, error) loads one — reject ragged rows, any other character, and patterns with no grid rows at all.

The grid is a fixed rectangle, and Mode is the whole reason I'm rewriting this: Bounded means everything outside the rectangle is permanently dead, Toroidal means both axes wrap (right edge borders left, bottom borders top). Standard rules, no variants: a live cell survives with exactly 2 or 3 live neighbours, a dead cell is born with exactly 3, everything else dies, all updates simultaneous per generation.

API I need on *Grid: Width() int, Height() int, Population() int, Alive(x, y int) bool (x is the column, y is the row, 0,0 top-left; in Toroidal mode ANY ints are legal and wrap — Alive(-1, 0) is the right edge — while Bounded just answers false outside the rectangle), Step() for one generation, StepN(n int) for n generations (n <= 0 does nothing), and Render() string giving the rows as '.'/'#' lines joined with \n, no trailing newline — exactly the shape the fixture rows come in.

lifegrid_test.go is the acceptance contract and it is picky on purpose: it pins exact frames for the blinker and toad oscillators, checks the still lifes don't twitch in either mode, walks a glider all the way around a 6x6 torus back to its starting cells, and watches the same glider crash into a bounded corner and settle into a block. Don't touch the test file or testdata/; go test -race -timeout 30s ./... has to pass.
